thesis: Robinhood is evolving from a commission-free trading app into a diversified fintech ecosystem with growing crypto revenue, subscription services (Gold), and international expansion. The SPEC. BUY rating reflects strong brand loyalty among retail investors, crypto momentum, and recurring revenue growth, offset by stretched valuation and regulatory risks.

drivers: Crypto revenue surpasses $1B annually with staking and custody expansion
Robinhood Gold subscribers double, validating recurring revenue thesis
International markets (UK/EU) contribute material revenue by 2027
Resolution of SEC Wells notice removes regulatory overhang
HOOD re-rated as fintech-crypto hybrid rather than traditional broker

assumptions: Modest margin expansion as subscription revenue mix increases
Crypto revenue grows but remains cyclical, not explosive
International expansion proceeds at measured pace
Valuation compresses slightly from current levels as growth normalizes

risks: SEC enforcement action or PFOF ban materially impacts revenue
Crypto bear market compresses transaction revenue and sentiment
Competition from Schwab, Fidelity, and Coinbase erodes market share
Rate cuts compress net interest income faster than trading recovers
Valuation multiple compression on growth deceleration

catalysts.event: Crypto product expansion (new tokens, staking, custody)
EU market deepening with local licensing and marketing
Potential resolution of SEC Wells notice
Fed rate cuts potentially boosting trading activity

key_risks: Regulatory / SEC enforcement risk from Wells notice and PFOF scrutiny
Crypto volatility and cyclicality of transaction revenue
Competition from traditional brokers and crypto-native platforms
Stretched valuation with limited margin for error
Interest rate sensitivity of net interest income
